title: Bitcoin Has Lost Nearly Half Its Value in 11 Months, Investors Revisit Their Reasons for Owning It
summary: Bitcoin has lost nearly half its value in 11 months, forcing investors to question their long-term strategies and the fundamental reasons for holding the cryptocurrency. The decline has sparked discussions about Bitcoin's role as a store of value and hedge against inflation.
body:
Bitcoin has lost nearly half its value over the past 11 months, according to a CNBC report from June 12, 2026. This significant drop has forced investors to revisit why they own the cryptocurrency.

The price decline has prompted a broader conversation about Bitcoin's utility. Investors are questioning whether Bitcoin still serves as a reliable store of value or a hedge against inflation, given its recent volatility. Others are looking at long-term trends and historical data to determine if this is a temporary setback or a broader market shift.

For everyday investors, this price drop highlights the risks associated with cryptocurrency investments. Bitcoin's volatility can lead to substantial gains, but also significant losses. This recent decline serves as a reminder to diversify portfolios and not to invest more than one can afford to lose.

Moving forward, investors should keep an eye on market trends and regulatory developments. Understanding the underlying factors driving Bitcoin's price, such as institutional adoption, regulatory changes, and technological advancements, can help inform better investment decisions. The CNBC report notes that the current environment is forcing investors to reassess their thesis for holding Bitcoin in the first place.
